prompt:
We print a word-hunt puzzle in the club newsletter and I'm tired of hand-checking submissions against the answer key. I need a checker, class Boggle in Boggle.java, plain Java, stdlib only.

Boards are 4x4 and live in text files (see grids/): four rows of four tiles separated by spaces. A tile is a single capital letter, except the Q cube whose face is literally 'Qu' — a bare 'Q' (or 'QU', or anything lowercase or multi-letter) is not a valid tile and parse must throw IllegalArgumentException, same for wrong row/column counts. Static Boggle.parse(String) builds a board.

Word matching is the classic rule set: a word is on the board if you can trace it through tiles that are adjacent horizontally, vertically or diagonally, never stepping on the same tile twice in one word. The Qu tile always spends both letters — it matches the letter pair QU in the word, a word can't use just the Q half of it, and note the same spelled letter on two different tiles is of course fine to use twice. Matching is case-insensitive.

API: boolean contains(String word) — pure path test, any length. static List<String> dictionary(String text) loads a word list (one word per line, trims, skips blanks, uppercases, keeps file order). List<String> findAll(Collection<String> words) returns every distinct dictionary word of length >= 3 that's on the board, alphabetically sorted, each word once no matter how many paths or how often it appears in the input. static int score(String word): the usual table — 3 or 4 letters score 1, five score 2, six score 3, seven score 5, eight or more score 11, and anything shorter than three scores 0. Scoring counts LETTERS of the word, not tiles touched (a word through the Qu tile is one tile longer than the cells it uses). int totalScore(Collection<String>) sums the scores of findAll. String report(Collection<String>) prints one 'WORD score' line per found word in sorted order and then a 'total N' line, every line newline-terminated.

words.txt is our master list; grids/letters.grid and grids/quveil.grid are last month's boards. TestMain.java pins the exact solutions for both — 'java TestMain.java' is the acceptance gate. Tests, grids and the word list are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 6: Boggle word search. Both pinned findAll lists (36 and 33 words) and totals (40/41) were derived BY HAND cell-by-cell before the reference existed; reference then passed 22/22 first run. Pins: 8-neighbor adjacency, no-cell-reuse (DOLED single-D, ERODE same-E-both-ends, NOON), distinct-cells-same-letter allowed (CEDE), Qu tile law (QUIP/EQUIP positive, bare Q / mid-tile stop / QUIT negative), length scoring incl. Qu word-length-not-cell-count, min length 3, dedup + case-insensitive matching, report grammar. Domain check: no existing boggle/word-hunt seed; ts/py word games are wordle/hangman (different surface).
